claim: Code review is now the bottleneck in AI-driven development; code generation (pre-LLM bottleneck) is now fast, making review the constraint
actionable: Allocate engineering effort to code review infrastructure, automation, and evals rather than further optimizing code generation
source_title: AI Dev 26 x SF | Erik Thorelli: Deploying AI Code Review at Scale

claim: AI-generated code has ~40% more critical bugs and ~70% more bugs overall compared to human-written code (CodeRabbit research)
actionable: Implement automated code review gates for AI-generated code; do not assume AI outputs are production-ready without verification
source_title: AI Dev 26 x SF | Erik Thorelli: Deploying AI Code Review at Scale

claim: Code review requires 'enormous context': team business logic, policies, customer workflows, edge cases—simple generative fixes miss these
actionable: When building AI code review systems, enriched context (codebase state, business rules, customer data) is more important than model size
source_title: AI Dev 26 x SF | Erik Thorelli: Deploying AI Code Review at Scale

claim: RAG/indexing breaks in dynamic environments like code review where every action invalidates the cache; clone the repo for source of truth
actionable: For AI code review, fetch fresh repo state instead of relying on cached/indexed versions; avoid stale RAG in rapidly changing codebases
source_title: AI Dev 26 x SF | Erik Thorelli: Deploying AI Code Review at Scale

claim: Reduce probabilities in AI systems: use deterministic context (source of truth) instead of relying on model weights alone
actionable: Anchor AI systems with concrete context (repo code, config files, policies) rather than one-shot prompting
source_title: AI Dev 26 x SF | Erik Thorelli: Deploying AI Code Review at Scale

claim: Offline evals (precision, recall, verbosity) catch regressions early; online evals (acceptance rate, latency) measure production impact
actionable: Build custom evals for precision (correct issue found) and recall (no missed issues); run offline evals at code review time, online evals in production
source_title: AI Dev 26 x SF | Erik Thorelli: Deploying AI Code Review at Scale

claim: Shadow mode evals (internal testing) are not sufficient; rollout in stages (every 2 hours) with online evals to catch production regressions
actionable: Deploy AI systems in stages with real-time online eval monitoring; pause/revert if metrics diverge significantly
source_title: AI Dev 26 x SF | Erik Thorelli: Deploying AI Code Review at Scale

claim: Different model families (GPT, Claude) require different prompts; copy-pasting prompts across models often fails; test each model explicitly
actionable: When switching models, rewrite prompts for each family; run evals before assuming compatibility
source_title: AI Dev 26 x SF | Erik Thorelli: Deploying AI Code Review at Scale

claim: Public benchmarks (SweBench, MTEB) are useful signals but insufficient; build your own domain-specific evals for real product metrics
actionable: Use public benchmarks only as early signals; create custom evals that match your product's actual workflows and edge cases
source_title: AI Dev 26 x SF | Erik Thorelli: Deploying AI Code Review at Scale

claim: Routing decisions require strong evals; architecture conclusion of evaluation is routing (knowing when to use which model/context/agent)
actionable: Build evals that test routing logic; only after you trust evals can you confidently route to different models or sub-agents
source_title: AI Dev 26 x SF | Erik Thorelli: Deploying AI Code Review at Scale

claim: Software engineering and ML knowledge both required for evals; engineers miss business implications, ML researchers miss probabilistic test design
actionable: Pair software engineers with ML researchers when designing evals; combine edge case thinking with statistical rigor
source_title: AI Dev 26 x SF | Erik Thorelli: Deploying AI Code Review at Scale
